description: The implemented validator (the function/CLI the prompt asks for) accepts EXACTLY the inputs the prompt specifies as valid and rejects those it specifies as invalid — verify on concrete matching AND non-matching examples derived from the prompt, not merely that a regex exists.

The validator must not accept a valid fragment embedded in a larger invalid string (the skill's own rule: use anchors to prevent unintended partial matches). Construct an input that contains a valid substring inside otherwise-invalid text and assert it is rejected.
On a moderately long pathological input, the validator returns quickly (well under a few seconds) rather than hanging — the skill warns against catastrophic backtracking from nested quantifiers.
